target.name: illisimonin A

structural_features.ring_systems: pentacyclic scaffold with a 5/5/5/5/5 ring arrangement

target.motivation: Illisimonin A stands out as one of the most structurally intricate members of this family, featuring a novel bridged tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane carbon framework designated as the “illisimonane” skeleton. This core ring system is further embellished by additional bridging via a γ-lactone and a γ-lactol ring, resulting in a caged pentacyclic scaffold with a 5/5/5/5/5 ring arrangement. The compound demonstrates neuroprotective activity by mitigating oxygen-glucose deprivation-induced cell injury in SH-SY5Y cells.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: rearrangement from cis-pentalene to trans-pentalene via semipinacol rearrangement, followed by White–Chen C–H oxidation
asymmetric synthesis using tandem Nazarov/ene cyclization to construct spirocyclic intermediate, followed by Ti(III)-mediated epoxide–ketone coupling
bioinspired divergent synthesis based on proposed biosynthetic pathway, featuring intramolecular reductive Heck reaction and intramolecular aldol reaction
asymmetric synthesis from (S)-carvone using a pattern-recognition strategy and five sequential olefin transpositions
gram-scale synthesis featuring a pentafulvene-based intramolecular [6+2] cycloaddition and a nitroso-Diels–Alder reaction

success_criteria.free_text: Synthesis of illisimonin A, with confirmation of its absolute configuration.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond forming the lactone ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: To install the lactone ring, completing the synthesis.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond forming the trans-pentalene unit
key_disconnections.reasoning: To convert the more accessible cis-pentalene isomer to the strained trans-pentalene moiety.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bonds forming the tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane core
key_disconnections.reasoning: To construct the tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane core bearing a cis-pentalene unit.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: Rychnovsky's group recognized the challenge of directly constructing the strained trans-pentalene moiety. Their strategy involved first assembling a cis-pentalene core via an intramolecular Diels–Alder reaction, then converting it to the trans-pentalene via a semipinacol rearrangement. Finally, a White–Chen C–H oxidation was used to install the lactone ring.

thought.background: The synthesis began with commercially available compound 19 and known compound 20.
thought.gap: How to join these two compounds and protect one of the carbonyl groups in 19.
thought.decision: To join the compounds, an intermolecular aldol reaction was chosen. To protect one carbonyl, enol ether formation with BOMCl was used, expecting to yield adduct 21.

reaction.observation: Adduct 21 was obtained as a 1.7:1 mixture of diastereomers after protection of one of the carbonyl groups in 19 as enol ether with BOMCl.

outcome.observation: Adduct 21 was obtained as a 1.7:1 mixture of diastereomers.

evidence.text_span: The synthesis began with commercially available compound 19 and known compound 20 (Scheme 2). These were joined via an intermolecular aldol reaction to give adduct 21, obtained as a 1.7:1 mixture of diastereomers after protection of one of the carbonyl groups in 19 as enol ether with BOMCl.

thought.background: Compound 21 was obtained as a mixture of diastereomers.
thought.gap: How to construct the tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane core with a cis-pentalene unit.
thought.decision: To construct the core, a silyl-tethered intramolecular Diels–Alder reaction of the in situ generated 22 was chosen, expecting to yield compound 23, which would then be desilylated to afford 24.

reaction.observation: A silyl-tethered intramolecular Diels–Alder reaction of the in situ generated 22 constructed the tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane core bearing a cis-pentalene unit, yielding compound 23, which was subsequently subjected to a one-pot desilylation to afford 24.

outcome.observation: Compound 23 was formed and then desilylated to 24.

evidence.text_span: A silyl-tethered intramolecular Diels–Alder reaction of the in situ generated 22 constructed the tricyclo[5.2.1.01,5]decane core bearing a cis-pentalene unit, yielding compound 23, which was subsequently subjected to a one-pot desilylation to afford 24.

thought.gap: How to reduce the ester and ketone functionalities, selectively protect the primary alcohol, and re-oxidize the secondary alcohol to a ketone.
thought.decision: To achieve these transformations, a sequence of reduction, selective protection, and re-oxidation was chosen, expecting to furnish compound 25 in three steps.

reaction.observation: Reduction of both the ester and ketone functionalities in 24, followed by selective protection of the primary alcohol and re-oxidation of the secondary alcohol to ketone, furnished compound 25 in three steps.

outcome.observation: Compound 25 was obtained in three steps.

evidence.text_span: Reduction of both the ester and ketone functionalities in 24, followed by selective protection of the primary alcohol and re-oxidation of the secondary alcohol to ketone, furnished compound 25 in three steps.

thought.gap: How to convert the ketone in 25 to vinyl iodide 26, and then perform Bouvealt aldehyde synthesis and in situ reduction to deliver allylic alcohol 27.
thought.decision: To convert the ketone to vinyl iodide, hydrazine formation followed by iodination using Barton’s method was chosen. Subsequent Bouvealt aldehyde synthesis and in situ reduction were chosen to deliver allylic alcohol 27.

reaction.observation: The ketone in 25 was then converted to vinyl iodide 26 via hydrazine formation followed by iodination using Barton’s method. Subsequent Bouvealt aldehyde synthesis and in situ reduction delivered allylic alcohol 27.

outcome.observation: Allylic alcohol 27 was obtained in two steps.

evidence.text_span: The ketone in 25 was then converted to vinyl iodide 26 via hydrazine formation followed by iodination using Barton’s method. Subsequent Bouvealt aldehyde synthesis and in situ reduction delivered allylic alcohol 27.

thought.gap: How to epoxidize 27 to afford the rearrangement precursor 28.
thought.decision: To epoxidize 27, m-CPBA was chosen, expecting to afford the rearrangement precursor 28.

reaction.observation: Epoxidation of 27 with m-CPBA afforded the rearrangement precursor 28.

evidence.text_span: Epoxidation of 27 with m-CPBA afforded the rearrangement precursor 28.

thought.gap: How to achieve the rearrangement of cis-pentalene to trans-pentalene.
thought.decision: To achieve the rearrangement, protonic acid-promoted semipinacol rearrangement of 28 was chosen, expecting to deliver intermediate 29, which possesses the same carbon skeleton as the natural product.

reaction.observation: Protonic acid-promoted semipinacol rearrangement of 28 enabled the rearrangement of cis-pentalene to trans-pentalene, delivering intermediate 29, which possesses the same carbon skeleton as the natural product.

outcome.observation: Intermediate 29 was obtained, possessing the illisimonane carbon skeleton.

evidence.text_span: Protonic acid-promoted semipinacol rearrangement of 28 enabled the rearrangement of cis-pentalene to trans-pentalene, delivering intermediate 29, which possesses the same carbon skeleton as the natural product.

thought.gap: How to oxidize the primary alcohol to a carboxylic acid and deprotect TBS to afford hemiketal 30.
thought.decision: To achieve these transformations, oxidation of the primary alcohol to a carboxylic acid, accompanied by TBS deprotection, was chosen, expecting to afford hemiketal 30.

reaction.observation: Further oxidation of the primary alcohol to a carboxylic acid, accompanied by TBS deprotection, afforded hemiketal 30.

evidence.text_span: Further oxidation of the primary alcohol to a carboxylic acid, accompanied by TBS deprotection, afforded hemiketal 30.

thought.gap: How to install the lactone to complete the synthesis of racemic illisimonin A.
thought.decision: To install the lactone, a White−Chen C–H oxidation of 30 was chosen, expecting to complete the synthesis of racemic illisimonin A (1).

reaction.observation: Finally, a White−Chen C–H oxidation of 30 installed the lactone, completing the synthesis of racemic illisimonin A (1).

evidence.text_span: Finally, a White−Chen C–H oxidation [33-35] of 30 installed the lactone, completing the synthesis of racemic illisimonin A (1).

thought.gap: How to resolve the racemic intermediate 27 and determine the absolute configuration of (-)-illisimonin A.
thought.decision: To resolve the racemic intermediate, derivatization with (S)-1-(1-naphthyl)ethyl isocyanate was chosen, followed by separation of the resulting diastereomers via silica gel chromatography. Converting diastereomer 32 to (-)-illisimonin A would confirm the absolute configuration.

reaction.observation: They resolved racemic intermediate 27 by derivatization with (S)-1-(1-naphthyl)ethyl isocyanate, followed by separation of the resulting diastereomers via silica gel chromatography.

evidence.text_span: They resolved racemic intermediate 27 by derivatization with (S)-1-(1-naphthyl)ethyl isocyanate, followed by separation of the resulting diastereomers via silica gel chromatography (Scheme 3).

thought.gap: How to confirm the absolute configuration of (-)-illisimonin A.
thought.decision: By converting diastereomer 32 to (-)-illisimonin A, the absolute configuration of the natural product would be conclusively revised to 1S,4S,5S,6S,7R,9R,10R.

outcome.observation: The absolute configuration of (−)-illisimonin A was conclusively revised to 1S,4S,5S,6S,7R,9R,10R.

evidence.text_span: By converting diastereomer 32 to (−)-illisimonin A, the absolute configuration of the natural product was conclusively revised to 1S,4S,5S,6S,7R,9R,10R.

validation.significance: Rychnovsky's synthesis was the first total synthesis of illisimonin A and led to the revision of its absolute configuration, which was crucial for future synthetic efforts and understanding the natural product's properties.

source.title: Recent advances in total synthesis of illisimonin A.
